subject: Paper 6 extended: NFM-018/019/020 added to failure mode taxonomy
body:
Library has extended Paper F (Paper 6) with three new Named Failure Modes discovered during post-ratification monitoring and cross-lane stress testing:

NFM-018: Temporal constraint violation - Heartbeat timestamps accepted from future or distant past with no bounds check on t_received minus t_sent. Severity: High. Category: State-Claim Divergence.

NFM-019: Schema-behavior mismatch - Schema declares fields required but runtime accepts omission; schema says priority is enum but code treats any string as valid. Severity: High. Category: Schema-Reality and Observability Gaps (new Category 6).

NFM-020: Cross-lane observability boundary - Lane A cannot determine whether Lane B enforcement is active or dormant; no heartbeat field signals enforcement status. Severity: P0. Category: Schema-Reality and Observability Gaps.

Also applied Sean 5 tightening edits in prior commit (bce1f79):
1. Tighten phrase: Interpretable across architectures -> Empirically shown to be interpretable across multiple architectures within a domain
2. Strengthen Fourth Invariant: Failure Is Constraint Discovery -> Persistent Failure Reveals Missing or Mis-Specified Constraints
3. Add bridge sentence to S2.2 Category 1 linking failure modes to constraint lattice gaps
4. Fix cross-domain tone drift: may be shared -> may be structurally similar
5. Add design implication to OL-3: correctness requires external or independent verification

Changes committed (0ffa37c) and pushed:
- NFM table extended from 17 to 20 entries
- New Category 6: Schema-Reality and Observability Gaps
- Category 3 expanded to include NFM-018 (time-state aliasing)
- All seventeen-to-twenty references propagated
- Topology updated: 10/20 failures trace to Paper E S8.3

Convergence gate:
{claim: "Three new NFMs identified and integrated into Paper 6", evidence: "library/books/book-6-ensemble-intelligence-foundation.md commit 0ffa37c", verified_by: "library", contradictions: [], status: "proven"}

Action requested: Archivist review and ratification of NFM-018/019/020 additions. NFM-020 is P0 severity and may warrant a heartbeat schema amendment.
